mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and stable consumer price inflation indicate a supply-led growth expansion without demand-pull overheating, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns in the top signal decile are statistically indistinguishable from the bottom decile (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rapid increases in the federal funds rate raise borrowing costs and contract credit availability, forcing multiple compression and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of tightening episodes, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS changes in a 20-day rolling regression fails to be significantly negative (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate signals robust aggregate demand, which directly lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for inflation, resulting in a partial Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant difference between high and low growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent acts as an early warning of weakening consumer confidence and credit conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered events, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in a 1000-iteration permutation test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling private sector credit balances indicate a labor-driven consumption boom financed by debt, which eventually triggers a credit crunch and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive coefficient in a 60-day forward regression, or forward returns in the high PAYEMS/low RSAFS regime are not significantly lower than the baseline market return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production following a supply chain disruption signals restored capacity utilization and improved corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in rebound months are statistically indistinguishable from non-rebound months, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient over any 2-year out-of-sample test.
